codeReview.summary: The modal save flow and markdown export are strong improvements, but loading a saved view does not actually restore the saved search filter to the active results. That misses the feature’s promise to restore all filter settings.

issues.description: `applyView()` copies `filters.searchQuery` into `searchQuery` but never updates `debouncedSearch`. Because App filtering uses `debouncedSearch`, loading a saved view with a saved search term updates the header input but leaves the result set unfiltered until the user types again. A direct store check after `applyView({ filters: { searchQuery: "iran" } })` leaves `debouncedSearch` empty.
issues.suggestedFix: When applying a view, set both `searchQuery` and `debouncedSearch` (or route the update through the debounced setter and flush it) so the saved search filter takes effect immediately when the view loads.

summary: Fail. Saved views do not immediately restore saved search filters because `applyView()` leaves `debouncedSearch` behind.
